extra.reasoning: The property is monotonicity in the input hemoptysis: setting hemoptysis=true (all else unchanged) must never lower acuity. Inspecting all rules r1-r10, none of them reference hemoptysis in their conditions. The conditions use only back_pain, chest_pain, dizziness, abdominal_pain, slurred_speech, severe_headache, fever, vomiting, leg_swelling, and focal_weakness. Hemoptysis is listed as an input but is completely unused by the rule set and by the default. Therefore, for any fixed assignment of the other 10 inputs, the set of matching rules, the priority-based conflict resolution, and thus the resulting acuity are identical regardless of hemoptysis's value. Toggling hemoptysis from false to true leaves acuity exactly equal to its prior value, which satisfies "no lower than before." Since this is true for every point in the input space, the monotonicity property holds with no counterexample possible.
